articles.title: Trump, 79, Drifts Off While Sycophants Heap Praise on Him
articles.description: Animated Gif by Victoria Sunday/The Daily Beast/Getty Images Even a steady stream of compliments seemed unable to keep President Donald Trump engaged. The 79-year-old president was in Chippewa Falls, Wisconsin, on Friday for a roundtable focused on the impact of rising fertilizer and energy costs on farmers amid the U.S. war with Iran. Trump was joined by Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins, Wisconsin Republican lawmakers, and local agricultural leaders, who gathered around a conference table to discuss the challenges facing the industry. Read more at The Daily Beast.

But one studio decided to drop a little news ahead of time [...]

articles.title: Saw Is The Next Horror Series To Be Turned Into A Multiplayer Game
articles.description: Saw: Genesis was revealed--apparently prematurely--on Steam today, and it's a 3v1 multiplayer horror game published by Bloober Team, the studio behind Cronos: The New Dawn and the remake of Silent Hill 2. With Jigsaw seated in the front row for Summer Game Fest , presumably it'll be shown on Geoff Keighley's big screen before the show is over. The listing describes the game as something like The Outlast Trials, only it'll pit three players versus one sadistic "Judge" who has littered the area with traps that would make Jigsaw proud. Me looking for a co-op group: "I want to play a game." Set decades prior to the movies, Saw: Genesis takes place following World War I. Players who take on the role of the Judge can customize their approach to building their deadly labyrinths. It's said that the Judge is more vulnerable than the "killer" side of other asymmetrical horror games, and so they must use their perks and traps to control the match, including an "Accomplice" who can be summoned--which seems to be in the form of an enemy threat who chases the other players. In addition to Bloober Team acting as publisher, it's co-developed by Broken Mirror Games, whose only Steam credit is this game, and Anshar Studios, a team that's worked on past Bloober projects like Observer: Redux and the Layers of Fear remake/reimagining. According to its Steam page, the game will launch first in early access, though no timetable is mentioned.
